mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth, producing higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS 3-month growth signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive-signal months do not outperform baseline 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above its prior 12-month high, tighter monetary policy raises discount rates and restrains risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month maximum
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed non-triggered returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not have lower 60-day forward returns than untriggered periods at p <= 0.10 or if sign accuracy is below 50% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts decline below its 12-month average by more than 10% signals weakening construction activity and household durable demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST drawdown trigger is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in consumer prices and increases in the federal funds rate indicate inflation-driven tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation rate rises while FEDFUNDS increases over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction term is not negative in walk-forward tests or if triggered 20-day returns are not below baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous gains in industrial production and payroll employment signal a broad expansion in output and income, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive INDPRO and PAYEMS state does not outperform other states over 20 trading days or if the interaction coefficient is nonpositive out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP signals falling aggregate demand and earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days after the contraction is observable.
failure_condition: Reject if observable GDPC1 contraction signals are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases or if drawdowns are not larger than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in industrial production signals near-term improvement in factory output and cyclical earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO growth triggers above +0.5% fail to produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of cases or if average triggered returns are negative.
